15.reason: The strcmp function does not guarantee returning -1; it returns a negative value, so this comparison may fail. Use < 0 instead.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The strcmp function does not guarantee returning -1; it returns a negative value, so this comparison may fail. Use < 0 instead.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The strcmp function does not guarantee returning -1; it returns a negative value, which may not be -1. This can cause incorrect ordering.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  strcmp returns any negative value for less-than, not only -1; should be <0
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [strcmp returns negative, not specifically -1; should be <0]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  strcmp returns a negative value, not necessarily -1, when the first string is lexicographically smaller. It should be < 0.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  strcmp returns a negative value when the first string is lexicographically smaller, not necessarily -1. Should use < 0

74.reason: [c is char; comparison with EOF may never succeed, leading to infinite loop]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  using char for fgetc result can cause infinite loop on systems where char is unsigned; should be int
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [c is char; comparison with EOF may never succeed, leading to infinite loop]

97.reason: If the input file is empty, head is NULL and cntWord is 0, this block creates a node with an uninitialized word. It should check cntWord != 0.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  for empty file, head==NULL and cntWord==0, yet this creates a node with uninitialized word; should also check cntWord!=0
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  If the input file is empty, head is NULL and cntWord is 0, this block creates a node with an uninitialized word. It should check cntWord != 0.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Should also check cntWord != 0 to prevent creating a node with an empty string if the file contains no valid words

68.reason: [c should be int to hold EOF; char may cause infinite loop on EOF]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [c should be int to hold EOF; char may cause infinite loop on EOF]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  c should be declared as int because fgetc returns int; comparing a char with EOF can cause an infinite loop if char is unsigned.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  c should be declared as int to correctly store and compare the return value of fgetc() with EOF
